title: Every checklist-* criterion in this Core Features dimension passes during the agent verifier run
No console.error or unhandled promise rejections appear in the browser log during the agent verifier session
Users collection supports create, edit, and delete with list rows and KPI counts updating from shared application state
At least two distinct views exist (Operations Overview and All Users) switchable from the sidebar without full page navigation
Domain state beyond CRUD is implemented: status badges, role assignment, list filters/sort that recompute visible users, and bulk status/role/delete on selected rows
Renders visible validation/feedback when Create user is submitted with empty required fields, and the users count does not increase
Users list is static HTML only with no working create/edit/delete against shared state
App chrome navigates via real outbound links to other origins instead of in-app view state
Redux Toolkit holds the users collection, active view, filters/selection, and theme — shared across components
Implemented with Stack: React + Redux Toolkit + Tailwind CSS; no browser storage APIs for persistence
Uses localStorage or sessionStorage to persist users, theme, or filters
Manages the users collection only in component-local useState rather than Redux Toolkit shared store
Imports or uses a disallowed external component library beyond DaisyUI / trendchart for core UI
Operations Overview keeps an asymmetric 12-column mosaic with a primary inverse Revenue run rate card; Users views use dense admin tables/forms
Role and status use distinct badge treatments; empty list state is visually present when the collection or filter result is empty
Lays out Operations Overview as a uniform equal-width single-column card stack with no mixed mosaic spans
Required hover animations are present on buttons, table/list rows, and sidebar nav; theme swap and popovers animate as specified
View switches update the main canvas without full reload and keep interactive chrome hover feedback
Omits hover washes on buttons, rows, or sidebar items so interactive chrome feels static on pointer hover
